latest.title: AGM Trading Update
latest.content:
15 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1874I
Mission Group PLC (The)
15 June 2026
15 June 2026
The Mission Group plc
("MISSION", the "Company" or "Group")
AGM Trading Update
Trading in line with expectations, strong Client retention and wins, continued focus on strategic delivery
Ahead of its Annual General Meeting held today,
MISSION
Group plc (AIM:TMG), a collective of sector-leading Creative, Sports and MarTech Agencies, provides the following update on trading for the period 1 January to 15 June 2026.
Trading in the current financial year remains in line with the Board's expectations. Against an ongoing backdrop of wider macroeconomic uncertainty this has been driven by continued strong Client retention, new Client wins and the benefits of the strategic actions completed earlier in the year to simplify and strengthen the Group's operating platform.
New Client wins were achieved across the Group's five practice areas: Integrated Marketing, Sports & Events, Property, PR and Healthcare. These included Westminster Council, Puma, Amaala Yacht Club, PwC, the International Tennis Federation and Volleyball World.
Good progress continues to be made against the key strategic priorities for 2026 outlined in the Group's full year results in March: US expansion execution, agency integration, consultancy offer launch, growth engine activation, capital allocation discipline and redefining the
MISSION
proposition. This includes further expansion of the pipeline of Client opportunities in the US within the Group's Mongoose Sports & Events practice. In addition, to further strengthen the combined capabilities of the Group's Integrated Marketing practice, Bray Leino was pleased to welcome globally recognised creative leader Wayne Deakin into the role of Chief Creative Officer.
A further update on trading will be provided, as usual, in a half year update in July.
ENDS
John Carey, Chief Executive
Giles Lee, Chief Financial Officer
Via Houston
The
MISSION
Group PLC
Simon Bridges/Andrew Potts/Harry Rees
Canaccord Genuity Limited
(Financial Adviser, Nominated Adviser and Broker)
020 7523 8000
Peter Tracey
Blackdown Partners Limited
(Financial Adviser)
020 3807 8484
Kate Hoare/Charlie Barker
Houston
(PR advisers)
077 3303 2695 / 0204 529 0549
E:
mission@houston.co.uk
NOTES TO EDITORS
MISSION Group is a collective of specialist agencies delivering marketing, communications and digital services for Clients across the UK and internationally. The Group combines deep sector expertise with connected capabilities across brand, digital, customer engagement, sponsorship, media, social, technology and communications.
Find out more at
www.themission.co.uk
Change of Accounting Reference Date
As previously announced, as a result of the change in the Group's accounting reference date, the Group's financial year will now end on 30 September annually, with its reporting timetable as follows:
· publication of audited annual financial statements for the 12-month period to 31 December 2025, by no later than 30 June 2026 (published on 4 June 2026);
· publication of unaudited interim accounts for the 6-month period to 30 June 2026, by no later than 30 September 2026;
· publication of audited annual financial statements for the 9-month period to 30 September 2026, by no later than 30 March 2027;
· publication of unaudited interim accounts for the 6-month period to 31 March 2027, by no later than 30 June 2027; and
· publication of audited annual financial statements for the 12-month period to 30 September 2027, by no later than 31 March 2028.
From then, annual and interim results will be published each year for the 12 months to 30 September and 6 months to 31 March, respectively.

latest.content:

RNS Number : 8945G
Mission Group PLC (The)
04 June 2026
4 June 2026
THE MISSION GROUP plc
("MISSION", "the Group")
Notice of AGM and Posting of Annual Report
The
MISSION
Group plc (AIM: TMG),
the Brand Performance Group, comprising of digital marketing and specialist communications Agencies
,
announces that copies of the Annual Report and Accounts in respect of the year ended 31 December 2025 have been sent to shareholders.
The Company also announces that Notice of the Annual General Meeting and the associated Proxy Form has been sent to shareholders. The Company's Annual General Meeting will be held on Monday 15 June 2026 at 12 noon at the offices of the Company, Fourth Floor, The Manufactory, 1-8 Alfred Mews, London W1T 7AA.
The Annual Report and the notice of AGM are available now on the Company's website at

Mission Group PLC (The)
21 May 2026
21 May 2026
The Mission Group plc
("MISSION" the "Company" or the "Group")
DIRECTOR/PDMR SHAREHOLDINGS
The
MISSION
Group plc (AIM: TMG) the Brand Performance Group, comprising of digital marketing and specialist communications Agencies,
has been notified of the following dealings in the Company's ordinary shares of 10p each ("
Ordinary Shares
"):
·
On 19 May 2026, David Zwirn, a person closely associated with Claudine Collins, Non-Executive Director, purchased 144,000 Ordinary Shares at a price of 13.99p per Ordinary Share.
·
On 20 May 2026, Jon Kempster, Non-Executive Director, purchased 68,852 Ordinary Shares at a price of 15.26p per Ordinary Share. His beneficial holding is 68,852 Ordinary Shares.
·
On 21 May 2026, Claudine Collins, Non-Executive Director, purchased 87,793 Ordinary Shares at a price of 17p per Ordinary Share. Her beneficial holding including those Ordinary Shares held by persons closely associated with her is 231,793 Ordinary Shares; and
·
On 21 May 2026, John Carey, Chief Executive Officer, purchased 175,587 Ordinary Shares at a price of 17p per Ordinary Share. His beneficial holding is 200,587 Ordinary Shares.

company_30s.what_they_do: TMG is a specialist marketing and branding agency that delivers digital campaigns, social media strategy, and creative services to consumer‑facing brands. It works across e‑commerce, lifestyle, and retail sectors, helping clients build online presence and drive sales.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The consumer‑lifestyle market is growing fast as brands shift spend from traditional media to data‑driven digital touchpoints. TMG’s niche focus on creative content and analytics gives it a competitive edge in a crowded agency landscape, making it a key partner for brands that need high‑impact online campaigns.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a rebuilding phase after a sharp fall in share price. Recent actions include a share buy‑back, a new growth‑share scheme, and several board changes aimed at tightening governance. The AGM and interim results suggest management is actively working to restore confidence, but the business remains cash‑burn heavy and still needs to hit profitability targets.

rns_narrative: TMG has been busy shuffling its leadership and capital structure: a board reshuffle, a growth‑share scheme to incentivise staff, and a share buy‑back to signal confidence in the business.

Financially, the company is reporting quarterly results that show a modest improvement in cash flow but still fall short of earnings expectations. The AGM updates highlight ongoing challenges in the consumer & lifestyle segment and a plan to tighten margins.
Collectively, these moves paint a picture of a company trying to stabilise its operations and regain investor trust while still navigating a volatile market environment.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – investors are listening but not yet convinced.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices point to the share buy‑back and growth scheme as signs that management believes the business can rebound. They see the recent board changes as a step toward stronger governance.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter focuses on the company’s ongoing cash burn, weak earnings, and the possibility that the buy‑back is a short‑term stunt that won’t change fundamentals.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from neutral to mildly bullish after the AGM, but overall chatter remains sparse, indicating that most market participants are still waiting for a clear turnaround.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – there is no public buzz, which means a catalyst could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Release of the full AGM financial statements – a better-than‑expected cash flow or margin improvement could spark a bounce.
Completion of the share buy‑back – a visible reduction in shares outstanding may lift the price.
Early uptake of the growth‑share scheme – if employees start exercising options, it signals confidence and could support the stock.
Any regulatory or partnership announcement that expands the client base, especially in the high‑growth e‑commerce space.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sharp volume spike at a key support level, a clear earnings surprise, or a sudden lift in the share buy‑back schedule. These signals would confirm that the market is starting to see value in the company again.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a recovery from a deep crash. The APEX score indicates tight compression, so the next move will likely be a breakout from the current low. Confirmation will come from a sustained uptick in volume and a price break above the recent low.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: After a deep plunge, the market has already taken the worst of the price fall. The buy‑back and growth scheme suggest that management believes the business can return to pre‑crash levels. If the price recovers to even half the pre‑crash range, the upside is significant while the downside is capped by the already‑taken loss.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still faces high cash burn and weak earnings. If the AGM results show a continued decline or if the share buy‑back stalls, the price could fall further. A continued lack of profitability could also trigger a re‑evaluation of the business model.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside far exceeds the remaining risk. The price is already 70‑95% lower than its peak, so a 50‑100% rebound would produce a large return, while the maximum loss is limited to the current deep trough.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks the recent low with at least 5‑10% volume above average, size the position to 5‑10% of capital, and set a stop 10% below the breakout level to protect against a false move.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small position until the AGM results are announced. If the earnings beat expectations or the buy‑back schedule accelerates, consider a quick entry.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy signals. The deep crash and lack of terminal risks keep the case bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TMG’s deep crash and quiet market sentiment create a high‑reward, low‑downside opportunity that could ignite once AGM results or the buy‑back confirms management’s confidence.
